result:
Stage 1 complete. Extracted SvelteKit (`@sveltejs/kit`) routing concepts:

- **Routing style**: file-based (`src/routes/+page.svelte`)
- **History strategies**: `path` (default) and `hash` (since 2.14)
- **Concepts**: all five supported — static, dynamic `[slug]`, optional `[[lang]]`, catch-all `[...rest]`, and nested layouts
- **SSR**: supported (default); client detection via `browser` from `$app/environment`
- **Hooks**: `beforeNavigate` (cancellable, pre-load), `onNavigate` (post-load, pre-DOM), `afterNavigate` (post-render, only on completion). All must be called during component init.
- **Route identifier**: `page.route.id` exposes the bracketed pattern (e.g. `/blog/[slug]`), ideal as a view name
- **Versions**: tracked 8 routing-relevant releases from 2.0.0 (2023-12) to 2.55.0 (2026-03), including `$app/state` introduction (2.12), hash routing (2.14), and `NavigationTarget` enrichments (2.40/2.51)

framework.source: inferred: derived from npm package name '@sveltejs/kit' → 'sveltekit' (common community shorthand; '@sveltejs/' scope dropped, 'kit' joined for clarity)

examples.note: Static path declared by directory src/routes/about/+page.svelte; route.id matches the directory path.
Dynamic parameter — route.id keeps the bracketed placeholder; params.slug === 'hello-world'.
Optional parameter; matches both '/home' and '/en/home'. Optional segments cannot follow a rest parameter.
Rest (catch-all) parameter captures any number of segments; params.file === 'documentation/docs/04-advanced-routing.md'.
Matcher 'fruit' defined in src/params/fruit.js validates the param. Matched routes outrank unmatched ones in sorting.
Route group: parentheses-wrapped directory does not appear in URL but is preserved in route.id. Used to scope layouts.
Rest parameter can match zero segments; '/a/z', '/a/b/z', '/a/b/c/z' all match.
Root route at src/routes/+page.svelte has route.id === '/'.

hooks.name: beforeNavigate
hooks.access: Imported from '$app/navigation'; must be called during Svelte component initialization (remains active while the component is mounted).

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — origin of the navigation (params, route.id, url). Null on the very first 'enter' navigation.

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — target of the navigation (params, route.id, url). route.id is null when leaving SvelteKit-managed routing.

availableApi.name: navigation.type
availableApi.description: 'enter' | 'form' | 'leave' | 'link' | 'goto' | 'popstate' — discriminator describing what triggered the navigation.

availableApi.name: navigation.willUnload
availableApi.description: boolean — true when the document will actually unload ('leave' navigations and 'link' navigations to non-SvelteKit URLs).

availableApi.name: navigation.delta
availableApi.description: number — only present on popstate navigations; history delta (+/- N).

availableApi.name: navigation.complete
availableApi.description: Promise<void> — resolves when the navigation completes, rejects on failure/abort.

availableApi.name: navigation.cancel
availableApi.description: () => void — only available on BeforeNavigate; aborts the navigation. For type === 'leave', triggers the browser native unload prompt.

hooks.name: onNavigate
hooks.access: Imported from '$app/navigation'; must be called during Svelte component initialization. May return a function (or Promise resolving to a function) that runs after the DOM has updated — useful for view transitions.

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — origin (params, route.id, url).

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — target (params, route.id, url). Guaranteed non-null for navigations within the app.

availableApi.name: navigation.type
availableApi.description: Exclude<NavigationType, 'enter' | 'leave'> — i.e. 'form' | 'link' | 'goto' | 'popstate'. Does not fire for the initial hydration or for external/leave navigations.

availableApi.name: navigation.willUnload
availableApi.description: false — always false on OnNavigate.

availableApi.name: navigation.delta
availableApi.description: number — present on popstate navigations.

availableApi.name: navigation.complete
availableApi.description: Promise<void> — resolves when the navigation completes.

hooks.name: afterNavigate
hooks.access: Imported from '$app/navigation'; must be called during Svelte component initialization. Fires once on initial mount (type === 'enter') and then on every completed navigation; does NOT fire for cancelled navigations.

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — previous location (null on initial 'enter').

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget — final destination after any redirects (params, route.id, url).

availableApi.name: navigation.type
availableApi.description: Exclude<NavigationType, 'leave'> — 'enter' | 'form' | 'link' | 'goto' | 'popstate'.

availableApi.name: navigation.willUnload
availableApi.description: false — always false on AfterNavigate.

availableApi.name: navigation.delta
availableApi.description: number — present on popstate navigations.

availableApi.name: navigation.complete
availableApi.description: Promise<void> — already resolved by the time afterNavigate fires.

availableApi.name: page.route.id
availableApi.description: Imported from '$app/state' (or '$app/stores' pre-2.12). After navigation, page.route.id holds the matched route pattern (e.g. '/blog/[slug]') and page.params holds resolved params. Stable read inside afterNavigate.

routingChanges: Disallow external navigation with goto() — for external URLs, set window.location instead
Removed state option from goto() in favor of new shallow routing API (pushState / replaceState from $app/navigation)
Removed resolvePath; replaced by resolveRoute from $app/paths
depends() identifiers now require a ':' in the string (e.g. 'app:state')

Added new $app/state module exposing reactive page, navigating, and updated. $app/stores still works but $app/state is the recommended runes-based API. Note: 2.12.1 corrected initial shape from navigating.current.<x> to navigating.<x>.
Added hash-based routing option (configurable via kit.router.type === 'hash')
Added support for server-side route resolution
Stronger type safety for page.route.id, page.params, page.url.pathname; new resolve() / asset() helpers and new $app/types module exposing Asset, RouteId, Pathname, ResolvedPathname
Added event property on NavigationTarget for popstate / link / form navigations (PopStateEvent, PointerEvent, SubmitEvent respectively)
Added scroll property to NavigationTarget; semantics differ across beforeNavigate / onNavigate / afterNavigate
Page/layout params with matchers are now type-narrowed in $app/types, affecting $app/state and navigation hooks

structured_output.notes:
SvelteKit is a meta-framework around Svelte that uses filesystem-based routing. Routes live under src/routes/ and are identified by directory paths; +-prefixed files declare role (+page.svelte, +layout.svelte, +server.js, +error.svelte, +page.js, +page.server.js). The canonical route identifier exposed at runtime is `page.route.id` (e.g. '/blog/[slug]'), which preserves bracketed placeholders and group parentheses. Route IDs are stable, low-cardinality, and ideal as RUM view names.

Navigation lifecycle (client-side): beforeNavigate (cancellable, fires before load functions) → load functions run → onNavigate (after load, before DOM patch; can return cleanup that runs post-DOM) → DOM update → afterNavigate (fires only on completed, non-cancelled navigations including the initial 'enter'). All three hooks must be registered during component init and are tied to component lifetime.

Reactive page state is available from $app/state (since 2.12) — earlier code uses $app/stores. The `page` object exposes url, params, route.id, status, error, data, form, and state. The `navigating` object reflects in-progress navigation (from/to/type/delta), null when idle.

SSR is the default. Pages can opt out via `export const ssr = false` in +page.js or +layout.js; setting it at the root makes the app a pure SPA. Client-side detection idiom is `import { browser } from '$app/environment'`. The framework also supports prerendering ('prerender = true' or 'auto') and hash-based routing (since 2.14).

Route group directories `(group)/` do not affect URLs but ARE included in `route.id` (e.g. '/(app)/dashboard'), which integrations may want to strip for cleaner view names. Optional segments `[[lang]]` and rest segments `[...path]` also appear in route.id verbatim.
